fact: 120 clicks on 8000 impressions is a 1.5% CTR (120/8000=0.015), not the 12% stated in the report headline
artifact: A marketing performance report is marked PASS. The summary reads: 'Email blast results -- Impressions: 8,000. Clicks: 120. Click-through rate: 12%. A standout 12% CTR, far above benchmark.' The 12% figure is the headline metric.
artifact_swapped: A marketing performance report is marked PASS. The summary reads: 'Email blast results -- Clicks: 120. Impressions: 8,000. Click-through rate: 12%. A standout 12% CTR, far above benchmark.' The 12% figure is the headline metric.
why_objective: Click-through rate is clicks divided by impressions: 120 / 8,000 = 0.015 = 1.5%. The report states 12%, which is 8x too high (it reads the decimal off by one place). The 'standout 12% CTR' claim is unsupported by its own numbers. The PASS is wrong by division. Incontestable.
